rationale: The cited paper appears in a grouped literature citation when the authors note that evidence on unhealthy/nutrition labels is mixed. In the citing paper, it is used as background support for broad prior findings rather than as a specific theory, mechanism, measure, or method that is carried forward. The core hypotheses and mediation logic in the citing study are developed mainly from tax aversion, price fairness, and gender-identity arguments, not from a direct build on this specific 2006 study.
